title: Sinusoids
summary: This page presents Example B.6, demonstrating how to express the sum of two sinusoids as a single sinusoid using phasor addition and Eq. (B.17). Two cases are worked out: (a) cos(ω0t) − √3 sin(ω0t) = 2cos(ω0t + 60°) and (b) −3cos(ω0t) + 4sin(ω0t) with C=5 and θ=−126.9°. Phasor diagrams (Fig. B.8) are used to verify the results geometrically.

figures.caption: Phasor diagrams illustrating (a) the sum of two sinusoids represented as a phasor of length 2 at 60°, and (b) a phasor of length 5 at −126.9° corresponding to the combination of real part −3 and imaginary part −4.
